description_long: This traffic barricade consists of two distinct barrier units, each constructed from metal frames with horizontal slats. The larger unit features three wide, weathered metal planks with diagonal orange and white reflective stripes, supported by four angled metal legs for stability. The smaller unit is topped with two yellow warning lights and has two horizontal slats, also marked with diagonal orange and white stripes. Both units show signs of wear, rust, and outdoor exposure, giving them a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The lights are mounted on the top corners of the smaller barricade, enhancing visibility in low-light conditions. The overall design is typical for urban roadwork or construction zones, intended to block or redirect pedestrian and vehicular traffic.
description: A pair of weathered metal traffic barricades with orange and white diagonal stripes, one featuring two yellow warning lights on top.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the angled metal legs and the back of the barricades, with the lights on the smaller unit visible from behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the metal slats, the angled support legs, and the prominent warning lights on the smaller barricade.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the arrangement of the legs and the alignment of the two barricades, with the lights and stripes partially visible.
description_view_3: Front view showing the full face of both barricades, the orange and white diagonal stripes, and the two yellow warning lights on the smaller unit.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, medium width, repeating across the horizontal slats.
